content.text: Fixed the mutation by cloning the style JSON early inside `Style._load()`. - Root cause: `Style.loadJSON()` passed the caller-provided object through to `_load()`, and `_load()` stores it as `this.stylesheet` and then mutates parts of it during initialization (notably affecting `projection` when `setStyle` is given a JS object). - Fix: in `src/style/style.ts`, `_load()` now does `clone(...)` on the incoming/transformStyle result *before* any validation or initialization, so internal mutations can’t affect the caller’s object. I added a vitest to reproduce the mutation and verified it passes after the fix, then deleted the test per instructions.

trajectory.result: Fixed the mutation by cloning the style JSON early inside `Style._load()`. - Root cause: `Style.loadJSON()` passed the caller-provided object through to `_load()`, and `_load()` stores it as `this.stylesheet` and then mutates parts of it during initialization (notably affecting `projection` when `setStyle` is given a JS object). - Fix: in `src/style/style.ts`, `_load()` now does `clone(...)` on the incoming/transformStyle result *before* any validation or initialization, so internal mutations can’t affect the caller’s object. I added a vitest to reproduce the mutation and verified it passes after the fix, then deleted the test per instructions.
